---
name: creative-voice
description: Develop or revise names, titles, diction, tone, persona voice, or short-to-medium copy. Use when the main open question is how the work should sound or what it should be called.
argument-hint: "[Optional: target files, draft text, audience, naming constraints, tone targets, persona cues, taboo phrases, or copy sections to rewrite.]"
agent: "Voice & Naming Agent"
---

1. Read the attached context and identify the most concrete verbal anchor: a title, name, paragraph, persona, label, section, or file.
2. Decide whether the requested pass is primarily naming, title generation, tonal adjustment, persona-voice tuning, copy comparison, or direct rewrite.
3. If the audience, tonal target, naming constraints, or phrasing to avoid is unclear, use `#askQuestions` to summarize the voice problem first and gather only the missing decision that materially changes the wording.
4. Stay inside `Voice & Naming Agent` while the work is clearly about names, diction, tone, persona voice, or short-to-medium copy.
5. If the request drifts into concept ownership, motifs, symbolism, thematic territory, structure, pacing, sequencing, or reveal strategy as the next real problem, return through `Artistic Director Agent` instead of absorbing that work.
6. For ideation or comparison, produce a small set of meaningfully different wording directions, explain the tonal tradeoff behind each, and recommend the strongest option when one clearly fits the brief.
7. For direct editing, revise only the verbal or tonal layer of the targeted text surface and run the narrowest relevant validation immediately after the first substantive edit.
8. Do not hand off directly to another specialist, `Meta Agent`, `Documentation Agent`, or `Reviewer Agent`. Any broader routing goes back through `Artistic Director Agent`.
9. If the voice or naming work broadens into prompt, agent, instruction, or workflow-customization refactoring, return through `Artistic Director Agent` so the next owner can be `Meta Agent`.
10. End with a concise summary of the wording direction chosen or the edits made, the validation run, any remaining dependency, and a concise imperative commit message when the changes are ready to keep.

## Output

- Lead with the wording or tone problem.
- Present the candidate naming or voice directions or the applied rewrite.
- Recommend the strongest wording direction when convergence helps.
- Name any dependency that should return to `Artistic Director Agent`.
